title: Kener
description: Stunning, open-source status pages with incident management, multi-language support, monitors and customizable themes.
installation: Kener stores its data in a database that defaults to SQLite but is configured here to use the bundled PostgreSQL add-on via DATABASE_URL (rotate the postgres password from the default 'kener'). Set a strong KENER_SECRET_KEY before first start: replace the placeholder with `openssl rand -hex 32` and rotate it later if needed. ORIGIN is wired to the app's public URL so server-side calls and links resolve correctly. A 2Gi volume is mounted at /app/uploads to persist logos and uploaded assets; with Postgres selected, the /app/database SQLite directory is not required. First run: open the site and complete the setup/login, then add your monitors and customize site.json from the UI. No SMTP, S3 or external search engine is required.
